page: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 3072.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix multiplication.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.metal-96xl demonstrates top-tier performance across multi-core processing and memory bandwidth tests. It achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 30918.01 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 1148.36 GB/sec for large blocks, while write bandwidth is recorded at 443.25 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 10%, with Redis handling 531.59 million operations per second.

The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps but does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs. While prompt processing for medium and large language models ranks in the top tier and strong categories respectively, text generation speeds are average to poor due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. Given its massive memory capacity, high-speed DDR5 RAM, and exceptional multi-core performance, the r8i.metal-96xl is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise-grade database operations, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and memory-intensive computational workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors, it delivers 384 vCPUs (192 physical cores) running at 3.9 GHz, alongside 3072.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory. The architecture features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 531.59 million operations per second. It features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Consequently, while LLM prompt processing is strong, text generation speeds remain average. This server is highly suited for large in-memory databases, complex data processing, and high-throughput multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs and 3 TB of DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory bandwidth with exceptional multi-core processing power for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores
3072 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and heavy multi-threaded workloads
